reason: Now rarer, no longer vendor-purchasable with Jeweller's Orbs, and the new non-white-socket bench crafts consume 5/20/75 of them, so both supply drops and a new sink appears at once.
The new default bench crafts burn 3 or 8 Chaos Orbs per reroll, adding a large early-league raw-chaos sink that props up chaos value against other currency.
Ultimatum becomes the only source, so supply collapses unless players spec into it while ring/amulet crafters still need them; the stack-to-20 change also makes bulk lots easier to move.
Now drops only from Abyss content, so belt supply is gated behind one mechanic while abyss-jewel builds still want them.

item: Abyssal Jewels (added spell damage / ailment effect rolls)
reason: Top-tier rolls were buffed (e.g. spell added damage and 20% ailment effect) at the same time supply became Abyss-exclusive, so well-rolled jewels should command a premium.
Massive buff (+2-4 cold gem levels, 80-140% cold damage, up to 50% freeze) lands in the same patch that buffs Freezing Pulse, Winter Orb and cold crit, making it the obvious budget cold-caster stick.
Rerolled onto new bases with level 30 purities, up to +100 life and 40% movement speed, but always corrupted — high rolls can't be re-divined into existence, so good corruption outcomes stay scarce.
Brand-new base with an all-elemental-resistances implicit; res-starved league-start rares and later crafts want it before supply of high-ilvl bases builds up.

item: High-ilvl caster staff bases (Imbued Wand class excluded; e.g. Judgement/Eclipse Staff)
reason: Staff caster prefixes were buffed to 200% spell damage, +4 gem-level mods and bigger added damage, so crafted caster staves and their fracture bases become a crafting target.
New currency that moves a stored map's divination card pool onto another map; card-farming strategies will chase it during price discovery before supply from the new Atlas cluster ramps.
New Legion-general drops that gamble a unique into another unique of the same class with a bonus implicit; gamblers and flippers will trade these heavily while odds are unknown.
Per-power-charge spell damage cut from 25% to 10-15% makes it far weaker on the charge-stacking builds that drove its price — expect demand and price to fall, do not stock.
The reworked unique-talisman beastcraft requires this specific beast, making it the bottleneck item for everyone chasing the new enchanted unique talismans.
